title: Neste and Fortum to Continue Energy Partnership With a New Bilateral Agreement
content: Neste, with production of renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel on three continents as well as production of high-quality oil products in Finland, and Fortum, one of the energy producers with the lowest specific COâ‚‚ emissions in Europe, are continuing their energy partnership with a new bilateral agreement for 2026-2030. Under the agreement, Fortum will supply Neste's Finnish operations with a total of approximately 2.6 terawatt hours (TWh) of electricity from Fortum's Nordic production portfolio. â€œNesteâ€™s refinery in Porvoo uses a significant amount of electricity, and securing the supply is critical for ensuring uninterrupted operation. This new agreement provides us with greater predictability while securing a stable electricity supply for the refinery,â€ says Jori Sahlsten, Executive Vice President of Oil Products at Neste. Fortum aims to advance the electrification of industry and society as part of the energy transition. The companyâ€™s target is that the hedged share of rolling 10-year outright generation volume is more than 25% by the end of 2028. The agreement with Neste is one of the milestones in reaching this target.
author: Analysts Account
